title: phycella arzae
intro: Phycella arzae is a type of flowering plant, also known as the "Desert Marigold," that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Phycella arzae include its ability to relieve symptoms of colds and flu, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Phycella arzae include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the plant's flowers and leaves in a carrier oil or water.
